HomeHero.badge: Technical partner for websites that should generate trust, leads and sales
HomeHero.description: I build landing pages, company websites, e-commerce projects and web apps for businesses that need more than a polished presence online. The goal is a digital asset ready for trust, traffic, SEO, ads and growth.

HomeAbout.title: How The Work Runs
HomeAbout.subtitle: I step in as a technical partner: shaping positioning, structure, execution and launch so the website works as a commercial tool rather than a decorative presence.
paragraphs: At the start, I help define the page logic: what belongs in the hero, where proof should appear, how the page leads to inquiry and which pages deserve separate treatment.
During the project, scope, priorities, structure and launch logic are fixed early. That reduces noise, cuts wasted iterations and gives the business control over the result.
At the end, the client gets more than code: a site prepared for ads, SEO, analytics, new pages and future growth.

skills.title: Frontend development
skills.description: React, Next.js and TypeScript for fast, stable and scalable interfaces.

skills.title: Backend development
skills.description: Node.js, APIs, databases and integrations for services, CRMs, forms and business logic.

skills.title: Product logic
skills.description: I translate business goals into page structure, content priorities and action flows that make the product commercially coherent.

skills.title: Technical SEO
skills.description: Page structure, metadata, schema.org and performance are built in from the start.

skills.title: Mobile-first
skills.description: Interfaces are designed to work well on phones, tablets and desktop without UX compromises.

process.title: Strategy and structure
process.description: We define the business goal, target audience, conversion logic and which blocks actually matter for decision-making.

process.title: Design and development
process.description: I turn the structure into a working interface, content hierarchy, integrations and user flows.

process.title: Launch and growth
process.description: I prepare the SEO base, analytics, forms, stable deployment and room for campaigns, discoverability and scaling after launch.

HomeOutcomes.title: What the client gets
HomeOutcomes.subtitle: Not just a finished website, but a clear plan, controlled delivery and a base for growth after launch.

items.title: A clear plan before kickoff
items.description: Before delivery starts, you understand the page structure, block logic and how the site is meant to drive inquiries or sales.

items.title: Defined scope and priorities
items.description: Scope, priorities and expectations are fixed early to avoid project noise, wasted iterations and a blurred budget.

items.title: A realistic launch path
items.description: The outcome is not abstract development work but a site or product prepared for campaigns, traffic, SEO or the next stage of growth.

items.title: A technical base without debt
items.description: Structure, performance, responsiveness, analytics and SEO are built in from the start instead of patched after release.

HomeServices.title: Services
HomeServices.subtitle: Each offer is framed around a business outcome rather than a feature list: leads, trust, sales, scale or a stronger launch path.
HomeServices.estimateTitle: Need an estimate for your case?
HomeServices.estimateDescription: Tell me what you sell, who your client is and what the website should achieve. I will help define the right format, scope and a realistic launch plan.

services.title: Landing pages
services.description: For paid traffic, demand testing or fast offer launches, when the website should drive one clear action instead of behaving like a brochure.

services.title: Corporate websites
services.description: For companies that need to look stronger, explain services with authority and turn qualified traffic into inquiries.

services.title: E-commerce stores
services.description: For businesses that want to sell online through a stronger storefront, a clearer checkout flow and a scalable catalogue structure.

services.title: Web applications
services.description: For SaaS, internal platforms, client portals and MVPs where a working product with logic and scalable architecture matters more than a generic website shell.

advantages.title: Outcome over feature list
advantages.description: Each service is packaged around what should change for the business after launch, not just what gets built.

advantages.title: Clear process
advantages.description: Scope, structure, technical stack and delivery priorities are defined early, so the project stays controlled.

advantages.title: Built for growth
advantages.description: Performance, SEO, analytics and future expansion are part of the initial system instead of patched in later.

HomeSeo.title: What makes the work commercially effective
HomeSeo.description: A strong web presence is not built from aesthetics alone. It works when structure, discoverability, messaging and technical execution support the same business goal.

pillars.title: Commercial structure
pillars.description: Pages, hierarchy and calls to action are built around decision-making, not just content placement.

pillars.title: Technical SEO
pillars.description: Metadata, schema, speed, internal linking and clean HTML support visibility without making the product feel mechanical.

pillars.title: Scalable system
pillars.description: The site is prepared for new pages, campaigns, services and content growth instead of being trapped in a one-off layout.

pillars.title: Performance discipline
pillars.description: Fast rendering, maintainable code and predictable production quality are part of the product standard.

HomeSeo.reasonsTitle: What this changes for a business
reasons: Cleaner user journeys and calls to action
A technical base that can support future growth
HomeSeo.ctaQuote: SEO should sit inside the product logic, not hang on the page like a checklist.
HomeSeo.ctaDescription: The work is strongest when design, content and implementation support the same commercial direction. That is how I approach product websites, service sites and custom web platforms.

HomeContact.title: Get an estimate for your project
HomeContact.description: Share the task, budget range and website goal. You will get a practical next step on structure, format and launch rather than a vague reply.
HomeContact.startTitle: What you get at the start
HomeContact.briefDescription: What needs to be built, for what kind of business and what the website should achieve.
advantages: A clear site plan before kickoff
A site prepared for SEO and ads

workMode.label: Engagement
workMode.value: Independent technical partner for commercial websites and digital products

workRhythm.label: Response rhythm
workRhythm.value: Reply within 24 hours with a practical next step

submit.success: Your message has been sent successfully!

fields.name: Name *
fields.messagePlaceholder: What do you sell, to whom, and what should the site achieve?
fields.messageHint: A short note on the task, business goal, timeline or expected outcome is enough.

ContactForm.formHint: Fill in the short request. Contact details, project format, budget and the core task are enough.

faq.title: Frequently asked questions
faq.subtitle: This block answers the main objections before kickoff: pricing, difference in approach, scope changes and what happens after inquiry.

items.question: Why can the price be higher than with a typical freelance developer?
items.answer: Because the work is not limited to implementation. I help shape page logic, structure, conversion flow, technical SEO readiness and launch direction. The client gets more than screens or code.

items.question: How is this different from just “building a website”?
items.answer: I work from the business task: what the site should prove, who it needs to persuade and what action it should drive. That changes the structure, copy priorities, case-study placement and CTA logic.

items.question: Can the project be made cheaper?
items.answer: Yes, by narrowing the scope. For example, we can start with one focused landing flow instead of a larger multi-page build, or move part of the integrations into a second phase.

items.question: What if the project changes during the process?
items.answer: That is manageable as long as the base scope is fixed before kickoff and new requests are handled separately. This keeps timeline, budget and delivery priorities under control.

items.question: Is SEO included in development?
items.answer: Yes. Baseline technical SEO is included: page structure, metadata, sitemap, schema, speed and clean markup. Dedicated SEO landing pages can be added as a separate next phase.

items.question: What do I get after sending an inquiry?
items.answer: You get a practical next step: an initial scope direction, a rough timeline view and a recommendation on the right project format for your case.

schema.name: Web development services
schema.description: Landing page development, business website development, e-commerce development, custom web applications and technical SEO.

hero.title: Services packaged around outcomes
hero.description: This is not a task list. It is a set of solution formats for different business goals: paid traffic launches, stronger trust, online sales, product logic or technical SEO readiness.

pricing.label: Pricing guide
pricing.title: A larger service menu for real business tasks
pricing.description: The price list is built around the work I can deliver well with React, Next.js and Astro: commercial websites, storefronts, CRM flows, payment integrations, relaunches and ongoing optimization. These are starting prices for a defined first scope, not abstract hourly estimates.

principles.title: Start from the first useful release
principles.description: Instead of inflating scope, I price the first commercially useful version: a launch-ready page, a working integration, a relaunch stage or an initial app module.

principles.title: Integrations are priced by business risk
principles.description: Payments, CRM sync and automation affect leads, orders and internal operations. Pricing reflects validation, edge cases, events, webhook handling and handoff quality.

principles.title: Astro is used where content speed matters
principles.description: For content-heavy and SEO-oriented projects, Astro can reduce overhead and improve performance, while Next.js stays the main option for application logic and dynamic flows.

categories.title: Launch websites and growth pages
categories.audience: For businesses that need a visible commercial presence quickly: traffic acquisition, service presentation, expert positioning or SEO landing expansion.

items.name: Landing page
items.stack: Next.js or Astro, responsive UI, analytics-ready setup
items.scope: Structure, page build, CTA flow, forms, events, baseline SEO and launch preparation.

items.name: Corporate / service website
items.scope: Homepage, services, about, cases, contact, trust blocks, metadata, schema and scalable page structure.

items.name: Astro content website
items.stack: Astro, content collections, light interactive islands
items.scope: Fast marketing or editorial website with high Lighthouse potential, clean content model and SEO-focused structure.

categories.title: Integrations and commercial operations
categories.audience: For teams that already have a website or product, but need payment, lead-routing or CRM workflows to work reliably instead of manually.

items.name: Payment gateway integration
items.stack: Next.js / React front-end with API or webhook layer
items.scope: Checkout connection, status handling, success/failure flows, event tracking and a safe handoff for finance or ops.

items.name: CRM integration and lead routing
items.stack: React / Next.js forms, CRM API integration, automation triggers
items.scope: Form-to-CRM mapping, source fields, event sync, lead segmentation and reduction of manual copy-paste work.

items.name: Website relaunch or migration
items.stack: Usually Next.js or Astro depending on content and dynamic needs
items.scope: Front-end rebuild, content migration path, redirects, metadata cleanup, performance reset and rollout planning.

categories.title: Stores, products and ongoing support
categories.audience: For businesses moving from a simple site to actual digital operations: storefront logic, dashboards, product interfaces and continuous technical improvement.

items.name: E-commerce storefront
items.stack: Next.js storefront, integrations with CMS, CRM and payments
items.scope: Catalogue, product pages, checkout flow, conversion UX, SEO-ready categories and integration foundation.

items.name: Web app / MVP module
items.stack: React / Next.js / TypeScript, API-connected product UI
items.scope: User flows, dashboards, roles, data states, API integration and a foundation for iterative product growth.

items.name: Technical SEO and monthly support
items.stack: Existing React, Next.js or Astro project
items.scope: Priority fixes, Core Web Vitals work, page updates, QA, analytics corrections and small ongoing improvements.

ServicesPage.outcomesPreviewTitle: What it changes for the business

consultation.title: Not sure which format will create the best result?
consultation.description: That is normal. The difference between a landing page, a company website, a store or a web app often becomes clear only after a short brief. I can help choose the right format without wasted budget or the wrong start.

ServiceDetailPage.valueDescription: The service is structured around what should change for the business after launch: sharper positioning, stronger trust, better inquiry flow, more sales or a stronger base for growth.
ServiceDetailPage.fitForTitle: When this service is the right fit
ServiceDetailPage.outcomesDescription: The expected result of this service: what changes in positioning, conversion flow, structure or growth-readiness once the work is delivered.
ServiceDetailPage.searchIntentTitle: Commercial queries worth building the page for
ServiceDetailPage.searchIntentDescription: For this service, the page should match real search intent. That is why I explicitly outline key phrasing, usage scenarios and the business value behind the offer.

estimate.title: Need an estimate for your exact case?
estimate.description: Tell me what you sell, who your client is, what the business goal should be and whether the site is meant for ads, SEO or product presentation. That makes it possible to suggest the right format quickly.

hero.title: Portfolio
hero.description: Selected case studies in website development, e-commerce and web applications, showing implementation quality, technical SEO thinking, judgment and production discipline.

seoText.title: What this web development portfolio shows
paragraphs: This portfolio is a selection of web development case studies, not just screenshots. It shows how I approach landing pages, service websites, multi-page corporate websites, e-commerce builds, product interfaces and technical improvements for existing products.

For a client, it matters not only how a screen looks but whether the developer can work with page structure, performance, technical SEO, integrations, forms, analytics and scaling. That is the level of execution I build into each release.

If you are reviewing a web development portfolio to evaluate website implementation quality, e-commerce execution, web application work and attention to detail, this archive gives a fast, practical view.

PortfolioProjectPage.resultTitle: What this created in the end
PortfolioProjectPage.challengeText: The project required more than a clean interface. The structure, messaging and technical foundation had to support the product or service in a commercially clear way.
PortfolioProjectPage.solutionText: The solution was built through a clear page structure, controlled front-end execution, strong content hierarchy and a dependable technical base for launch and future growth.
PortfolioProjectPage.resultText: The outcome is more than a set of screens. It is a digital product with stronger presentation, clearer interaction flow and a better base for SEO, campaigns or scaling.
PortfolioProjectPage.featureIntro: Key elements that shape the product and commercial value of this case.

PrivacyPolicyPage.title: Privacy policy
PrivacyPolicyPage.intro: This Privacy Policy explains how I, Serhii Oberemchuk, collect, use and disclose your personal information when you use my website.

collection.title: Information I collect
collection.description: I may collect different types of information, including:

items.personal: Personal information such as your name, email address and phone number when you submit forms on my website.
items.usage: Usage information such as IP address, browser type, visit time and interaction with the pages.
items.device: Device information such as device type, operating system and basic browser environment details.

cookies.title: Cookies
cookies.description: My website uses cookies to improve your experience. Cookies are small text files stored on your device while you browse the website.
cookies.moreInfo: More details about how I use cookies are available in the <cookiesLink>Cookie policy</cookiesLink>.

usage.title: How I use your information
usage.description: I use the information collected to:

items.provide: Provide, maintain and improve my website and services.
items.respond: Respond to your requests and communicate about your project.
items.analyze: Analyze website usage to improve the user experience.
items.protect: Protect against fraud, abuse and technical risks.

disclosure.title: Disclosure of information
disclosure.description: I may disclose your information in the following situations:

items.providers: To service providers who help me operate and maintain the website.
items.legal: If required by law or necessary to protect my rights.
items.transfer: As part of a transfer of assets or reorganization, with notice where appropriate.

rights.title: Your rights
rights.description: Depending on your jurisdiction, you may have the following rights regarding your personal data:

items.access: The right to access your personal information.
items.correction: The right to correct inaccurate or incomplete information.
items.deletion: The right to request deletion of your personal data.
items.objection: The right to object to processing in cases provided by law.

changes.title: Changes to this Privacy Policy
changes.description: I may update this Privacy Policy from time to time. The current version will always be published on this page.

contact.title: Contact
contact.description: If you have questions about this Privacy Policy, please contact me:

metadata.title: Cookie policy
metadata.description: Detailed information about how I use cookies on my website and how you can manage your preferences.

openGraph.title: Cookie policy | Serhii Oberemchuk
openGraph.description: Detailed information about how I use cookies on my website and how you can manage your preferences.

twitter.title: Cookie policy | Serhii Oberemchuk
twitter.description: Detailed information about how I use cookies on my website and how you can manage your preferences.

whatAreCookies.title: What are cookies?
whatAreCookies.description: Cookies are small text files stored on your device while you browse a website. They help the website function correctly, support analytics and remember your preferences.

usage.title: How I use cookies
usage.description: The website may use several categories of cookies: to keep the site working, for baseline analytics and, if introduced later, for future personalization or marketing.

types.title: Types of cookies used on the site

rows.name: Necessary cookies
rows.purpose: Required for the website to work correctly, preserve your preferences and support forms.

rows.name: Analytics cookies
rows.purpose: Help understand how visitors use the website and which pages perform best.

rows.name: Marketing cookies
rows.purpose: May be used for content or advertising personalization if such tools are introduced later.

items.consent: cookie-consent - stores your consent preferences for cookie usage.
items.session: session - supports stable site behavior and preserves temporary settings during a session.

items.ga: _ga - stores a unique identifier for baseline analytics. Duration: 2 years.
items.gaId: _ga_[ID] - used by Google Analytics to distinguish sessions and visits. Duration: 2 years.
items.gid: _gid - used for statistics within a 24-hour window.
items.gat: _gat - helps limit request rate. Duration: 1 minute.

marketing.title: Marketing cookies
marketing.description: At the moment the site does not actively use marketing cookies. If such tools are added later, this page will be updated.

thirdParty.title: Third-party cookies
thirdParty.description: Some cookies may be set by third-party services, for example analytics tools. For details, you should review the privacy policies of those services.

googleAnalytics.title: Google Analytics
paragraphs: The site may use Google Analytics for baseline traffic analysis. This service uses cookies to collect non-personal statistical information about page visits.
Information collected through Google Analytics is used to evaluate site performance and improve structure and content.
You can opt out of Google Analytics via the <optOutLink>Google Analytics opt-out page</optOutLink>.

management.title: How to manage cookies
management.description: You can manage your cookie preferences in several ways:
management.siteSettings: Through the site settings - you can update cookie consent when that mechanism is active on the site.
management.browserSettingsDescription: Most browsers let you manage cookies through built-in settings:

settingsCard.title: Update cookie preferences
settingsCard.description: If the consent management mechanism is active on the site, you can revise your cookie preferences at any time.

consequences.title: Consequences of disabling cookies
paragraphs: If you disable certain cookies, some parts of the website may not work as smoothly as intended.
Disabling analytics cookies does not block basic access to the site, but it reduces the ability to improve it based on real usage patterns.

changes.title: Changes to this Cookie Policy
paragraphs: I may update this policy from time to time. The current version will always be published on this page.
It is reasonable to review this page periodically to stay aware of changes in the approach to cookies.

contact.title: Contact
contact.description: If you have questions about this Cookie Policy, please contact me:

intro.title: 1. Introduction
intro.description: Welcome to the Serhii Oberemchuk website. By using this website, you agree to follow these terms. If you do not agree with any part of them, please do not use the website.

definitions.title: 2. Definitions
definitions.user: "User" means any person who accesses or uses the website.
definitions.content: "Content" means any information, text, graphics, images, video, audio, data and other materials published on the website.
definitions.services: "Services" means the services described on the website, including web development, web design, SEO and related technical solutions.

websiteUse.title: 3. Use of the website
websiteUse.description: By using the website, you agree to:
items: Use the website only for lawful purposes and without violating the rights of third parties.
Not perform actions that could damage, overload or destabilize the website.
Not attempt to gain unauthorized access to any part of the website or related systems.
Not collect information about other users without their consent.
Not use the website to distribute malicious software or for other abusive scenarios.

ip.title: 4. Intellectual property
ip.description: The website, its content, design, code, graphics and other materials belong to me or are used on lawful grounds. They are protected by copyright and related rights legislation.

orders.title: 5. Ordering services
paragraphs: If you submit an inquiry or brief through the website, you agree to provide accurate, current and sufficient information about the task.
Any cooperation regarding services is clarified separately: scope, timing, price and delivery terms are fixed through a separate agreement.

pricing.title: 6. Pricing and payments
pricing.description: Prices shown on the website are indicative. Final pricing depends on scope, technical requirements and required integrations.

liability.title: 7. Limitation of liability
liability.description: I am not liable for indirect, incidental or consequential damages that may arise in connection with the use of the website.
items: Your use of, or inability to use, the website.
Actions I take or do not take in response to your requests or messages.
Errors, interruptions, delays in operation or technical failures.
Unauthorized access to information you transmit.
Actions of third parties or external services linked from the website.

warranty.title: 8. Disclaimer of warranties
warranty.description: The website is provided "as is" and "as available". I do not guarantee uninterrupted operation, absolute security or complete absence of errors.

privacy.title: 9. Privacy
privacy.description: Your privacy matters to me. More about collection, processing and storage of data is described in the <privacyLink>Privacy policy</privacyLink>.

cookies.title: 10. Cookies
cookies.description: The website uses cookies to improve the user experience. More details are available in the <cookiesLink>Cookie policy</cookiesLink>.

changes.title: 11. Changes to the terms
changes.description: I may update these terms from time to time. The current version will always be published on this page, and continued use of the website means acceptance of the updated terms.

termination.title: 12. Suspension of access
termination.description: I may restrict or suspend access to the website in case of violation of these terms or for other justified reasons.

law.title: 13. Governing law
law.description: These terms are interpreted under applicable law, taking into account the jurisdiction in which cooperation is actually carried out.
